note: ABNT NBR 16149:2013, NBR 16150:2013, and NBR IEC 62116:2012 are confirmed real and active Brazilian standards used for INMETRO PV inverter homologation. Source URL (abntcatalogo.com.br) is the ABNT catalog portal — it exists and is official but paywalled; not the ideal authoritative link, however the publisher claim is accurate. The mandatory_status reference to 'Portaria INMETRO governing PV inverters' is generically correct — originally Portaria 004/2011, now superseded by Portaria 140/2022 (effective compliance deadline May 2025 for ≤75 kW). Gap content (60 Hz / 127-220 V vs 50 Hz / 220-380 V, no mutual recognition) is accurate. No material errors found.

ANEEL REN 1000/2021 confirmed as real and active (full PDF at aneel.gov.br/cedoc/ren20211000.pdf). REN 482/2012 correctly described as superseded. Lei 14.300/2022 (Marco Legal) confirmed at planalto.gov.br. Source URL (gov.br/aneel/pt-br/assuntos/geracao-distribuida) resolves and covers distributed generation with explicit REN 1000/2021 reference. CAVEAT: ANEEL's own page does not explicitly state INMETRO certification as a prerequisite — the INMETRO requirement flows from Portaria 140/2022 (product law), not directly from REN 1000 text. The row's framing that 'inverters must hold a valid INMETRO Certificate' is substantively accurate in practice but is an indirect regulatory chain, not a verbatim REN 1000 requirement. This is a nuance, not an error.

Portaria INMETRO 140/2022 is confirmed as the current governing instrument for PV inverter compulsory certification — it replaced Portaria 004/2011 with full compliance required by May 2025 (36 months from May 2022). The fragment's [UNVERIFIED] flag on the portaria number was prudent but the number 140/2022 is now confirmed correct. Standards (NBR 16149, NBR 16150, IEC 62116/62109) are confirmed in use for grid-tied inverter testing. HOWEVER: source URL (gov.br/inmetro/pt-br/assuntos/avaliacao-da-conformidade/programa-brasileiro-de-avaliacao-da-conformidade) returns HTTP 404. FIXED URL: use https://www.gov.br/inmetro/pt-br/acesso-a-informacao/perguntas-frequentes/avaliacao-da-conformidade/sistemas-e-equipamentos-para-energia-fotovoltaica/como-obter-pbe-compulsorio-para--sistemas-e-equipamentos-para-energia-fotovoltaico (confirmed live, official INMETRO FAQ on PV equipment compulsory certification).

Source URL (gov.br/inmetro/pt-br/assuntos/acreditacao/organismos-acreditados) confirmed live — lists accredited bodies with links to RBLE/RBC databases. ABNT NBR IEC 62109-1 and -2 exist as Brazilian adoptions of IEC 62109-1 and -2 (confirmed referenced in Portaria 140/2022 scope and industry test reports), though exact ABNT edition year is not confirmed from a primary ABNT source. Fragment correctly flags [UNVERIFIED] on exact NBR numbers. CGCRE as INMETRO's accreditation body is accurate. The description of ILAC-member foreign labs not being automatically accepted is consistent with INMETRO practice. No material errors; [UNVERIFIED] flags appropriate.

Two issues: (1) Source URL (gov.br/inmetro/pt-br/assuntos/avaliacao-da-conformidade/marca-de-conformidade) returns HTTP 404 — page does not resolve. FIXED URL candidate: https://www.gov.br/inmetro/pt-br/assuntos/avaliacao-da-conformidade (parent page) or the ABNT ebook at abnt.org.br/wp-content/docs/certificacao/Ebook-Selos-de-Identificacao-da-Conformidade.pdf. (2) Standards list cites 'Portaria INMETRO no. 301/2012 (Marca de Conformidade INMETRO — general seal usage rules)' — this is likely an error. Portaria 301/2011 is for automotive components; Portaria 301/2012 does not appear to cover general conformity seal rules. General INMETRO seal usage rules are governed by the RGCP (Regulamento Geral dos Programas de Conformidade) and INMETRO Portaria 274/2014, not a 'Portaria 301/2012'. The [UNVERIFIED] flag in the fragment was appropriate. The mandatory_status (INMETRO seal mandatory after certification) is substantively correct.

Source URL (gov.br/inmetro/pt-br/assuntos/regulamentacao/produtos-regulamentados) returns HTTP 404. FIXED URL: use https://www.gov.br/inmetro/pt-br/acesso-a-informacao/perguntas-frequentes/avaliacao-da-conformidade/sistemas-e-equipamentos-para-energia-fotovoltaica/como-obter-pbe-compulsorio-para--sistemas-e-equipamentos-para-energia-fotovoltaico (confirmed live). Standards cited (ABNT NBR 16149, NBR 16150) are correct. However, the row cites 'INMETRO Portaria 004/2011' as the current governing instrument — this was the original portaria but has been superseded by Portaria 140/2022 (effective May 2022, full compliance required May 2025). Portaria 004/2011 is no longer the current regulatory instrument; the row should reference Portaria 140/2022 as the operative regulation, with 004/2011 noted as the predecessor. The SISCOMEX import registration requirement and 'importer of record' requirement are accurate Brazilian import law requirements. mandatory_status is correct.

Source URL (gov.br/aneel/pt-br/assuntos/geracao-distribuida) confirmed live and covers REN 1000/2021 distributed generation rules. ANEEL REN 1000/2021 is confirmed (confirmed PDF at aneel.gov.br). Lei 14.300/2022 (Marco Legal) confirmed. PRODIST Module 3 reference is consistent with ANEEL practice. NB/T 32004-2018 cited as Chinese equivalent is plausible but [UNVERIFIED] flag from original author was appropriate. The four-gate compliance chain described (INMETRO OCP → ANEEL REN 1000 → concessionária approval → SISCOMEX) is accurate in substance. No material errors.

ABNT NBR IEC 62109-1 and -2 confirmed as Brazilian adoptions of IEC 62109-1 and -2, referenced in Portaria 140/2022 scope for safety requirements. Exact ABNT edition year not confirmed from primary ABNT source (catalog is paywalled) — fragment's [UNVERIFIED] flags are appropriate. Source URL (gov.br/inmetro/pt-br) is the INMETRO homepage — too generic to serve as a reliable source for this specific claim, but it resolves and is an official government domain. A more specific URL would be the Portaria 140/2022 news page: https://www.gov.br/inmetro/pt-br/centrais-de-conteudo/noticias/inmetro-atualiza-o-regulamento-para-equipamentos-de-sistemas-fotovoltaicos. mandatory_status [UNVERIFIED] flag is appropriate — Portaria 140/2022 compliance deadlines are May 2024 (≤10 kW) and May 2025 (10–75 kW), so for products already in market these deadlines have passed and enforcement is now active.
